questions.question: What is the default value of the CSS position property?
questions.explanation: The default value of position is static. Static elements are positioned in the normal document flow and do not respond to top, right, bottom, left, or z-index.

questions.question: An element with position: absolute is positioned relative to what?
questions.explanation: An absolutely positioned element is placed relative to its nearest ancestor that has a position value other than static. If no such ancestor exists, it uses the initial containing block.

questions.question: Which position value keeps an element fixed relative to the viewport even when scrolling?
questions.explanation: An element with position: fixed is positioned relative to the viewport and stays in the same place even when the page is scrolled.

questions.question: What does position: sticky do?
choices: Removes the element from the document flow permanently
Positions the element relative to the viewport at all times
Acts as relative until a scroll threshold is reached, then becomes fixed
Sticks the element to its parent's bottom edge
questions.explanation: A sticky element is treated as relative until its containing block crosses a specified scroll threshold (set with top, right, bottom, or left), at which point it becomes fixed within its container.

questions.question: The ___ property controls the stacking order of positioned elements that overlap.
questions.explanation: The z-index property sets the stack level of a positioned element. Higher values appear in front of lower values. It only works on elements with a position value other than static.

questions.question: An element with position: ___ is offset from its normal position but still occupies its original space in the flow.
questions.explanation: A relatively positioned element is offset from where it would normally be, but the space it originally occupied is preserved in the document flow.
